summary: Iteration 2 backend testing of SkiFi Designs admin auth, Stripe subscription fallback, and Resend email integration. All 29 pytest cases pass (19 new + 10 regression). Verified: admin login (wrong/correct password + 422 on missing), admin auth guard on /admin/submissions & /admin/me (no/invalid/malformed bearer all return 401), submissions list returns items + stats {total, paid, pending, revenue_usd}, payment_status filter works (both pending and paid), monthly_retainer falls back to mode='payment' with amount=999.0 when STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID is empty, per_slide x10 still returns amount=150.0, MongoDB payment_transactions docs include new 'mode' and 'emails_sent' fields with correct defaults, and _maybe_send_emails code path correctly flips emails_sent True with idempotent re-invocation.

minor.issue: ADMIN_SESSIONS is in-memory dict - all sessions are wiped on backend restart. Acceptable for single-instance dev but won't scale horizontally. Consider Redis/Mongo for production.

Revenue is computed via a separate find(amount only) + python sum. For >1000 paid docs this misses some. Better: db.aggregate([{$match:{payment_status:'paid'}},{$group:{_id:null,total:{$sum:'$amount'}}}]).
No rate-limiting on /admin/login — vulnerable to brute force. ADMIN_PASSWORD has decent entropy but adding slowapi or similar would harden it.

Race condition: emails_sent flag is set AFTER Resend send completes. Concurrent webhook + status-poll callbacks could double-send. Consider atomic findOneAndUpdate({emails_sent:false},{$set:{emails_sent:true}}) before sending.

action_items: (Optional, high-value) Add atomic flag-flip in _maybe_send_emails to prevent double-send race (findOneAndUpdate before sending)
(Optional) Replace revenue sum with MongoDB aggregation pipeline so it doesn't cap at 1000 paid docs
(Optional) Persist ADMIN_SESSIONS in MongoDB or Redis so sessions survive backend restarts
critical_code_review_comments: secrets.compare_digest used for password comparison — good, constant-time.
secrets.token_urlsafe(32) for session tokens — good entropy (256 bits).
Session expiry check correctly compares timezone-aware datetimes (datetime.now(timezone.utc)).
ADMIN_SESSIONS dict is process-local — sessions don't persist across restarts or scale.
require_admin dependency cleanly returns 401 for missing/malformed/expired/invalid tokens — verified all 4 paths.
MongoDB _id properly excluded from /admin/submissions via projection {'_id':0} — verified in test.
New 'mode' and 'emails_sent' fields on PaymentTransaction model & in DB — verified via direct mongo read.
Subscription fallback to one-time charge is well-isolated (elif pkg['type']=='subscription' branch) with billing_note in metadata — auditable.
_maybe_send_emails has idempotency guard (if tx.get('emails_sent'): return) but the read+write is not atomic — minor race risk.
Resend errors are swallowed (logger.exception) so a Resend outage won't break the payment flow — good defensive design.
send_payment_emails uses asyncio.to_thread to call sync resend SDK — good, doesn't block event loop.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ prefixed payment_transactions docs during testing (a mix of monthly_retainer and per_slide, all in 'pending' state). One fake 'paid' tx with session_id starting cs_test_emailpath_* was inserted and then deleted by the email-path test (clean teardown).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All admin + subscription-fallback + email-codepath features are working as specified in the review request. Test files at /app/backend/tests/ can be re-run as full regression (29 cases, runs in ~6s). pytest.ini enables asyncio mode=auto so future async tests don't need a marker. The test_maybe_send_emails_idempotent_codepath test exercises the real Resend integration through to the send call — Resend may log a 'send failed' for the unverified test recipient (Resend test mode only allows the account owner email), but the function still returns and the emails_sent flag still flips (verified). To test true Stripe subscription mode in future, set STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID to a real recurring Price ID — the code path is already wired (_create_subscription_session).
rca of the issue: No issues found — all 10 features from the review request validated by 19 dedicated test cases plus 10 prior regression cases, all green. (1) Admin login wrong→401 ✓, (2) correct→200 with token+expires_at ✓, (3) submissions w/o auth→401 ✓ (also invalid & malformed bearer→401), (4) submissions w/ token→200 items[]+stats{total,paid,pending,revenue_usd} ✓, (5) payment_status=pending filter ✓ (and paid filter only returns paid), (6) /admin/me w/ token→{authenticated:true} ✓, (7) monthly_retainer→mode='payment' fallback with amount=999.0 ✓ (STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID empty as designed), (8) per_slide x10→amount=150.0 ✓, (9) payment_transactions DB doc has 'mode' and 'emails_sent' fields confirmed by direct MongoDB read ✓, (10) _maybe_send_emails code path verified end-to-end with emails_sent flag flipping True + idempotent on re-call ✓. Regression on root /api/, /api/webhook/stripe, /api/status, /api/payments/status/{unknown}: all green.
